topic: OMNIX Local Kanban Board, Workspace Isolation, and Multiplexed Squads Router
context: We researched the fifth reference library, multica, to design a task-centric managed agent workflow, multi-workspace isolation, and agent squad routing.
options_considered: Run agents purely inside conversation tabs, requiring the developer to copy-paste prompts and manually watch raw console outputs.

Implement: 1. A local Task Kanban Board mapping issues through states (Todo -> In Progress -> Confirming -> Done), rendering execution timeline trees inside issue cards; 2. Strict Workspace-level isolation protecting paths, SQLite records, and environment variables; 3. Squads multiplexed router where a Leader agent parses, delegates, and coordinates subtasks to concurrent Teammates.
chosen_option: Implement local Kanban workspace, strict project isolation, and Squads multi-agent routing.
reasoning: Task-Centric Managed Flow: Modern software engineering is organized around tickets/tasks. Moving from 'chat sessions' to 'Kanban issues' lets developers assign work to AI agents and walk away, returning only when the Status Dock turns blue (Confirming) or green (Done).

Zero Cross-Project Leakage: Working on multiple client projects concurrently requires strict security. Restricting agents to a sandboxed workspace path and setting specific workspace-level database scopes prevents leaking API keys or files across project directories.

Squad Routing Orchestration: Complex refactoring tasks require modifying different layers (e.g. Frontend React UI vs Backend LibSQL). Assigning an issue to a Squad allows a smart Leader agent (e.g. Sonnet-based Claude Code) to route specific components to specialized sub-agents running in parallel, maximizing speed.
tradeoffs: Running multiple local CLI agents concurrently will stress system RAM and CPU: resolved by setting strict Max Concurrent Agent limits and enforcing the Rust Idle Reaper (reclaiming zombified agent RAM after customizable inactivity).
impact_scope: Frontend board rendering (React Kanban board, issue detail drawer)
Tauri workdir executor (environment variable injector, workspace boundaries)
future_risks: High concurrent CPU usage during parallel agent executions: mitigated by queuing agent tasks when CPU load exceeds a customizable threshold (e.g. 85%).
